### make_section_header

>  make_section_header (title:str, level:int=2, code_names:List[str]=None)

*Create a markdown section header with proper formatting.*

Wraps code/function names in backticks automatically.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>

<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>title</td>
<td>str</td>
<td></td>
<td>The section title.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>level</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>2</td>
<td>Header level (1-6), default 2.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>code_names</td>
<td>List</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Names to wrap in backticks in the title.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>NotebookNode</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>A markdown cell with the header.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

### has_standard_ending

>  has_standard_ending (nb:nbformat.notebooknode.NotebookNode)

*Check if notebook has the standard ending structure.*

Standard ending is: - \## Next (markdown) - \#| export (empty code
cell) - \## Export (markdown) - \#| hide +
`[`nbdev_export`](https://s01st.github.io/nbdev-mcp/11_tools/nbdev.html#nbdev_export)`
(code cell)

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 9%" />
<col style="width: 38%" />
<col style="width: 52%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>nb</td>
<td>NotebookNode</td>
<td>The notebook to check.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>bool</strong></td>
<td><strong>True if notebook has the standard ending.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

## Cell Insertion

Smart cell insertion with structure awareness.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/nbformat.py#L297"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### find_insertion_point

>  find_insertion_point (nb:nbformat.notebooknode.NotebookNode,
>                            cell_type:str='code')

*Find the best insertion point for a new cell.*

For code cells: - If notebook has standard ending, insert before the
‘\## Next’ section - Otherwise insert before ‘\## Export’ if it exists -
Otherwise append to end

For import cells: - Insert after the Imports section header

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>nb</td>
<td>NotebookNode</td>
<td></td>
<td>The notebook.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>cell_type</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>code</td>
<td>Type of cell being inserted: ‘code’, ‘markdown’, or ‘import’.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>int</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>The index where the new cell should be
inserted.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/nbformat.py#L347"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### insert_cell

>  insert_cell (nb:nbformat.notebooknode.NotebookNode,
>                   cell:nbformat.notebooknode.NotebookNode,
>                   position:str='auto', after_index:Optional[int]=None,
>                   is_import:bool=False)

*Insert a cell into a notebook with smart positioning.*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>nb</td>
<td>NotebookNode</td>
<td></td>
<td>The notebook to modify (in place).</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>cell</td>
<td>NotebookNode</td>
<td></td>
<td>The cell to insert.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>position</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>auto</td>
<td>Where to insert: ‘auto’ (smart), ‘end’, or ‘start’.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>after_index</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Insert after this specific index (overrides position).</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>is_import</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>False</td>
<td>If True, treat as an import cell (insert in imports section).</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>int</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>The index where the cell was inserted.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

## Structure Enforcement

Functions to ensure notebooks follow standard structure.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/nbformat.py#L486"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### ensure_standard_ending

>  ensure_standard_ending (nb:nbformat.notebooknode.NotebookNode)

*Ensure notebook has the standard ending structure.*

Adds the standard ending (Next, empty export, Export, nbdev_export) if
not already present.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 9%" />
<col style="width: 38%" />
<col style="width: 52%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>nb</td>
<td>NotebookNode</td>
<td>The notebook to modify (in place).</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>bool</strong></td>
<td><strong>True if the ending was added, False if already
present.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/nbformat.py#L522"

>  fix_header_formatting (nb:nbformat.notebooknode.NotebookNode)

*Fix markdown headers to use backticks for code names.*

Detects function/class names in headers and wraps them in backticks.

### standardize_notebook

>  standardize_notebook (path:pathlib.Path, write:bool=True)

*Apply all standard formatting to a notebook.*

- Ensures standard ending structure
- Fixes header formatting

<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />

## Export
